name: Pearl Harbor National Memorial
tagline: The USS Arizona Memorial, Battleship Missouri, and the site where December 7, 1941 changed the world — free admission, book ahead.
parking: $7/day in visitor center lot — arrives early, fills fast. Do NOT leave valuables in your car (high-theft area)
hours: Daily 7am–5pm. Last Arizona Memorial boat at 3:30pm. Closed Thanksgiving, Christmas, New Year's Day
bestTime: Arrive by 7am for walk-up tickets or book online 8 weeks ahead on Recreation.gov ($1 service fee). Mornings are cooler and less crowded
talkStory: The USS Arizona still leaks about a quart of oil per day — sailors call them 'black tears,' and the slick is visible from the memorial platform. The structure was designed by Alfred Preis, an Austrian-born architect who was himself interned as an enemy alien after the attack. Its dip-and-rise shape symbolizes initial defeat and ultimate victory. Arrive by 7am for walk-up tickets or book on Recreation.gov eight weeks ahead. NO BAGS ALLOWED — only wallets, phones, cameras, and water bottles. Plan a full day if visiting all four Pearl Harbor sites.
